title: Acosta cineraria
intro: Acosta cineraria, also known as Patagonian rose, is a shrub-like plant native to the Andes mountains. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Acosta cineraria include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acosta cineraria are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
